texts.preview_tail: : Try-Catch blocks are used to catch exceptions during runtime but @ExceptionHandler is more like middleware that catches the exception at controller level. This way you don't have error handling code
texts.full_text: multiple exceptions then it becomes cumbersome as you have to write separate catch blocks with different exception types On the other hand using @ExceptionHandler annotation allows us: - To handle all kind of errors at one place - Provide a consistent error message for each type or category of request and response. This can be useful when we need send an email, log in our system etc. In conclusion: Try-Catch blocks are used to catch exceptions during runtime but @ExceptionHandler is more like middleware that catches the exception at controller level. This way you don't have error handling code
